meta.notes: Single source of truth for all camera settings. Field cards and PWA are generated from this file.

global_settings._comment: Set once, never change. Apply across all modes.

display.histogram: DISP button mode — not a menu setting. Press DISP to cycle to Histogram view.
display.level_gauge: DISP button mode — not a menu setting. Press DISP to cycle to Level view.
display.menu_path: MENU → (Shooting) → Shooting Display (for grid lines only)

custom_buttons.AF-ON: Subject Recog. AF (hold to activate Eye AF)
custom_buttons.menu_path: MENU → (Setup) → Operation Customize → Custom Key/Dial Set.

MR1.name: Landscapes
physical_setup: Tripod: recommended for slow shutter speeds
Turn SteadyShot OFF if on tripod

field_notes.why_f8: Sweet spot for sharpness. f/5.6–f/11 range. Avoid f/16+ (diffraction blur).
field_notes.why_iso100: Base ISO = cleanest image. Camera controls shutter speed.
field_notes.when_to_adjust: Clouds moving fast? Increase shutter speed in dial. Very dark? Allow ISO up to 400.

MR2.name: Wildlife
physical_setup: OSS switch ON (lens) — controls stabilization, overrides camera menu

field_notes.why_manual_auto_iso: Lock aperture (DoF) and shutter (freeze motion). ISO is the flexible variable. ISO AUTO Min. SS does not apply in Manual mode — shutter is already fixed.
field_notes.why_f63: Maximum aperture at 350mm = maximum light. No choice here.
field_notes.why_1000: Stops most perched/walking animals. Dial UP to 1/2000-1/4000 for birds in flight.
field_notes.when_to_adjust: Bird in flight → shutter to 1/2000 or 1/4000. Low light perched bird → shutter to 1/500.

field_notes.why_aperture_priority: Walk-around doesn't need fast shutter. Pick aperture (DoF), let camera handle rest.
field_notes.why_f56: Sharp, versatile, decent DoF. Not so narrow it loses light; not so wide backgrounds blur too much.
field_notes.when_to_adjust: Dark market → allow wider aperture (f/2.8). Want background blur for a person → f/2.8.

M1.name: Portrait / People
M1.recall_method: MENU → (Shooting) → Shooting Mode → Recall Camera Setting → M1
physical_setup: Mount Sigma 18-50mm f/2.8, set to 50mm for tighter framing
OR Sony 70-350mm at 70mm for compressed portrait

field_notes.why_f28: Widest available on 18-50mm = maximum bokeh. Subject sharp, background soft.
field_notes.why_eye_af: Eye AF locks focus on the eye — most important part of a portrait.
field_notes.future_upgrade: Sigma 56mm f/1.4 (~$400) or Sony 50mm f/1.8 (~$250) for stronger bokeh.

M2.name: Low Light / Dusk-Dawn
M2.recall_method: MENU → (Shooting) → Shooting Mode → Recall Camera Setting → M2

settings._aperture_note: Saved with 11mm mounted. Camera clips to lens minimum if a slower lens is used.

physical_setup: Mount lens with widest aperture for the scene

field_notes.why_wide_open: In low light, gathering light is priority #1. DoF is secondary.
field_notes.why_1_60_min: Handheld limit. Below 1/60 = camera shake with wide lenses.
field_notes.when_to_adjust: Static scene on a surface → drop to 1/30 or 1/15. Moving people → keep 1/60.

M3.name: Night Sky / Stars
M3.recall_method: MENU → (Shooting) → Shooting Mode → Recall Camera Setting → M3

settings._peaking_note: Peaking is a global setting (already configured: On, Mid, Red). Not slot-specific.

physical_setup: Focus: zoom in on bright star in live view → rotate focus ring until star is pinpoint → lock

field_notes.why_f18: Gathers 2.25× more light than f/2.8. Stars need every photon.
field_notes.why_20s: 500 rule: 500 / 11mm = 45s max. 20s is conservative for sharp, round stars.
field_notes.why_iso3200: Starting point. Dark sky (no moon, no city) → try ISO 1600. Light-polluted → ISO 6400.
field_notes.why_daylight_wb: AWB makes stars orange. Daylight = neutral whites.
field_notes.why_long_exp_nr_off: Long Exp NR blocks camera for 20+ seconds between shots.
field_notes.why_2s_timer: Pressing shutter causes vibration that blurs stars. 2s lets vibration settle.
field_notes.when_to_adjust: Moon up → shorten to 10s. Very dark sky → try 25s. Check star shape: if trailing, shorten.

M4.name: Video — Wildlife
M4.recall_method: MENU → (Shooting) → Shooting Mode → Recall Camera Setting → M4

field_notes.why_180_shutter_rule: Shutter at 2× frame rate = natural motion blur. 30p → 1/60s. 60p → 1/125s.
field_notes.why_active_steadyshot: Video benefits from maximum stabilization. Walking/panning with long lens is hard.
field_notes.slog3_note: If color grading in post: switch Picture Profile to S-Log3. Flat/grey on screen is normal.
field_notes.why_different_from_mr2: Video has different shutter speed rules, stronger IS needs, and video-specific AF behavior.
